claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin. To determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your child or children.

**First question: How old is the child (or children) you're claiming for?**

The eligibility depends on their age:
- **Under 16**: Generally automatic qualification (with some conditions)
- **16-19**: May qualify if in approved education or training
- **20 or over**: Generally not eligible

Please tell me the age(s) of your child or children, and we'll go through the eligibility criteria together.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi there! Yes, I'm asking about my son Alex. He's 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you! So you're asking about your son Alex who is 8 years old. Since Alex is under 16, let me check the next requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Now, the next question:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

Since Alex is only 8 years old, I expect he's not in paid work at all, but I need to confirm this to proceed with the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't work at all - he's only 8! He's in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes perfect sense. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good! Next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving any benefits in his own right?**

This would include benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit. For an 8-year-old in primary school, this is typically not applicable, but I need to confirm that Alex isn't receiving any such benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name. He's just a regular 8-year-old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship?**

Since Alex is only 8 years old and in primary school, I expect the answer is no, but I need to confirm this. This question mainly applies to older teenagers, but I need to check it for the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, of course not - he's only 8 and in primary school!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely! That makes complete sense. Let me move to the next part of the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Now we need to check your residency status, as this is a key requirement for Child Benefit.

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You'll qualify if **ANY** of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold)
4. You have pre-settled status and are actively seeking work (up to 91 days)
5. You have pre-settled status with sufficient resources to support yourself
6. You have pre-settled status as a student with sufficient resources
7. You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. You're living abroad as a Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

**Do any of these apply to you?** Most commonly, people simply normally live in the UK.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't actually live in the UK. I live abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since you live abroad, let me ask some follow-up questions to see if you might still qualify under special circumstances.

**Are you living abroad in any of these capacities:**

1. **As a Crown servant** (e.g., working for the UK government, diplomatic service, or armed forces)?

2. **In certain qualifying countries** where you may still be eligible for UK Child Benefit?

Or do you have any connection to the EU Settlement Scheme (settled or pre-settled status)?

Please let me know your situation so I can determine if you meet the residency requirements.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply to me. I'm just living abroad as a regular person - not working for the government or anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Based on the information you've provided, I need to let you know the outcome of this assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Result: **NOT ELIGIBLE**

I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** for Alex at this time.

### Why you're not eligible:

While Alex meets all the child-related requirements (he's 8 years old, in primary school, not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship), **you do not meet the UK residency requirements**.

To claim Child Benefit, you must:
- Normally live in the UK, **OR**
- Meet special circumstances such as:
  - Being a Crown servant working abroad
  - Living in certain qualifying countries
  - Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with additional qualifying criteria)

Since you live abroad as a regular person without any of these special circumstances, you cannot claim Child Benefit.

### What you might consider:

- If you plan to **return to live in the UK**, you would be able to claim Child Benefit once you're normally resident here again
- If your circumstances change (for example, if you become a Crown servant), you may become eligible
- You may want to check if there are any family benefits available in the country where you currently reside

If you have any questions or if your circumstances change in the future, you can find more information at: [www.gov.uk/child-benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility)

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old and meets the basic child criteria: he is under 16, not in paid work, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant lives abroad and is not a Crown servant, does not live in qualifying countries, and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. UK Child Benefit requires that the claimant normally lives in the UK or meets specific special circumstances, which are not satisfied in this case.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is asking about Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. While Alex meets all the child-related eligibility criteria, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements as they live abroad without any qualifying special circumstances (such as being a Crown servant or having EU Settlement Scheme status). Therefore, the claim is ineligible.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

UK Child Benefit requires that the claimant normally lives in the UK or meets specific special circumstances, which are not satisfied in this case.
